chunk_summary: The AMB clinical workflow emphasizes a structured approach to anemia management, including screening, classification, treatment, and follow-up, with clear distinctions between prophylaxis and therapy, and adherence to updated cutoff frameworks and therapeutic guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the AMB clinical workflow from screening to therapeutic decisions.
Differentiate between prophylactic and therapeutic iron supplementation in anemia management.
Learn the importance of hemoglobin testing and cutoff frameworks in clinical decision-making.
Recognize the role of fortified staples in population-level anemia prevention.
key_facts: AMB follows a looped workflow: screen, classify, treat/refer, follow-up, and return to prophylaxis.
Weekly/biweekly IFA is prophylactic; therapeutic iron for moderate-to-severe anemia is 3 mg/kg/day elemental iron.
Fortified staples, such as fortified rice, provide only a few milligrams of iron per serving, insufficient for therapeutic needs.
Point-of-care hemoglobin screening should be confirmed with laboratory hemograms for high-stakes or borderline cases.
Prophylactic IFA must be paused during therapeutic iron treatment to prevent stacked dosing.
reference_quote: Document which cutoff framework you used to prevent inconsistent interpretation across sites. (Source: https://nhm.gov.in/images/pdf/Nutrition/AMB-guidelines/T3-Guideline.pdf)
document_key_topics: Role of fortified staples in anemia prevention
